pages.full_text:
Our campsite was basic – only one shower, but clean. A man next to us was camping on his own with 4 children
__Friday__ Boulanger 2 min. walk. Eve bought cakes.
	We picked up M+H & drove past Chateau de Chambord.
	We drove north, Charles map-reading with the GPS through Paris suburbs. Just south of Amiens, St. Fuscien, is Margaret's hotel. We dropped off her case & found a LeClerc & stocked up for England. Simon drove into Amiens & we found a very good place to eat supper, overlooking the boat-gardens, Hortillages. Lovely scenery.
	In the night there was a terrific thunderstorm, but Margaret slept through it!
~~Thursday~~ __Saturday 12th August '06__
	We all came into the "hotel" for a stupendous breakfast, artistically laid out –
pages.notes: The word 'Thursday' is crossed out and replaced with 'Saturday'. 'Boulanger' may refer to a bakery/shop rather than a place name but included as it appears in context. 'St. Fuscien' is a village south of Amiens. 'M+H' likely refers to Margaret and husband or two named individuals. Text continues from a previous page (mid-sentence at top).

pages.full_text:
Angel,
weeping on
the tomb of
Guillan Lucas,
Amiens
Cathedral.

The largest
Gothic Cathedral
in France.
75 stones high
on nave column
before vaulting
__XIII__

watermelon, apples & grapes, sugared (frozen?) [a bit.] musoli, bread, cake, croissants. We chatted about his design business & Chad.
	We looked at Amiens Cathedral & set
photo_captions: Angel, weeping on the tomb of Guillan Lucas, Amiens Cathedral. The largest Gothic Cathedral in France. 75 stones high on nave column before vaulting XIII
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in photograph of a cherub/angel statue at Amiens Cathedral. The annotation 'XIII' (underlined) beside the photo caption likely refers to a Roman numeral label or chapter/section number rather than a page number — it does not appear in the top outer corner. The word 'musoli' may be 'muesli'. 'frozen?' has a question mark in the original with a small note 'a bit.' written nearby. 'Guillan Lucas' could be 'Guillan' or another spelling — read as written. Text ends mid-sentence ('& set'), continuing to next page.
